title: What Is In Iran’s 10-Point Proposal, and How Does It Compare to U.S. Demands?
summary: The plan, which reasserts Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz and maintains the country’s right to nuclear enrichment, is not the same as the one President Trump said was a “workable basis” for negotiations.

title: What Trump Has Accomplished in Iran After 5 Weeks of War
summary: President Trump said “we have already met and exceeded” his military objectives. But his goals are largely unresolved.

title: Gas Prices Won’t Quickly Return to Prewar Levels, Even if the Strait of Hormuz Reopens
summary: Some wells can be turned on in days or weeks, but bringing the Gulf’s energy system back to something akin to normal will take months.

title: Georgia, Wisconsin Elections Show Declining Appetite for Republican Candidates
summary: A Republican won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s seat, but Democrats shifted the district 25 points to the left since the 2024 presidential race. Conservative candidates lost in Wisconsin, too.

title: Stung by Voters, Republican Legislators Move to Curb Citizen Initiatives
summary: After citizens in Republican states used ballot measures to protect abortion, expand Medicaid and raise the minimum wage, statehouses are moving to make such initiatives much harder.

title: In Georgia’s 14th District Special Runoff, Democrats Pulled Off Their Biggest Shift Yet
summary: Shawn Harris lost by about 12 percentage points in the 14th Congressional District, but he shifted the district 25 points to the left.

title: Who Is Rex Heuermann, the Man Accused in the Gilgo Beach Serial Killings?
summary: Mr. Heuermann was a successful architectural consultant who lived on Long Island with his family. He was arrested in 2023.

title: Who Are the Women Rex Heuermann Killed in the Gilgo Beach Murders Case?
summary: Six of the eight women whom Mr. Heuermann said he killed worked as escorts and advertised on sites like Craigslist and Backpage.

title: The 25 Best Restaurants in Chicago
summary: We scouted the city’s vast food scene, from renowned Italian beefs to tavern-style pizza to Michelin-starred tasting menus. (And we’re here for your comments.)

title: The Best Restaurants in Philadelphia
summary: The dining scene here is having a moment, whether it’s outstanding pizza, fiery Ethiopian fried chicken or French inflected cuisine that feels like a cozy dinner party.

title: The Best Restaurants in Boston
summary: It’s not just seafood and Italian in the greater Boston area. There’s great Vietnamese, omakase, Peruvian and bánh mi worth seeking out. (Don’t worry, there’s also great seafood and Italian.) We’ll see you in the comments.

title: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
summary: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the post.

title: Patients Are Using Chatbots to Fight Medical Bills, With Mixed Results
summary: While chatbots like Claude and ChatGPT can help narrow the information divide between patients and providers, they can also dispense flawed advice.

title: Meta Unveils New A.I. Model, Its First From the Superintelligence Lab
summary: The model, Muse Spark, performed better than Meta’s previous A.I. models but lags rivals on coding ability.

title: Dealer Known as Ketamine Queen Sentenced to 15 Years Over Matthew Perry’s Death
summary: Prosecutors say Jasveen Sangha’s customers knew her as the Ketamine Queen. She sold the ketamine that killed the “Friends” star in 2023.

title: 48% of New Yorkers Approve of Mamdani’s Performance, Poll Says
summary: The new poll from Marist also found that most New Yorkers believed the city was moving in the right direction under Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

title: How’s He Doing? 13 New Yorkers Weigh In on Mamdani’s First 100 Days.
summary: We talked to voters in neighborhoods where Zohran Mamdani is revered, in areas where he is feared and in diverse parts of the city where opinions differ.

title: Frankie Muniz Refuses to Stay in His Lane
summary: At 40, he is a father, a NASCAR driver and back as the star of a “Malcolm in the Middle” revival. “I have unfinished business,” he said.

title: An Audacious $724 Million Building Reinvents LACMA
summary: Two decades in the making, the David Geffen Galleries will offer an unconventional approach to art history and cement the director Michael Govan’s legacy.

title: A.I. May Worsen Wealth Inequality
summary: A.I. will further enrich the winners and impoverish the losers, with inevitable societal impacts.

title: Did Wokeness Leave Us Worse Off?
summary: The debate over words we can and can’t say.

title: Jeff Shell Steps Down as President of Paramount
summary: Mr. Shell, the former chief executive of NBCUniversal, is leaving after becoming entangled in a legal battle with a professional gambler, R.J. Cipriani.

title: Young New Yorkers Are Lining Up for Construction Apprenticeships
summary: Facing a bleak job market and fears around artificial intelligence, young workers are lining up for a shot to develop skills and secure jobs in the trades.

title: Long Island Rail Road Strike Looms, as M.T.A. and Unions Reach Impasse
summary: Workers could shut down America’s busiest passenger rail service beginning on May 16 if they do not reach a deal with transit officials.

title: Heat Wipes Out Western Snowpack, Raising Fears of Summer Drought
summary: A record-mild winter and blistering spring heat will mean higher risks of water shortages and wildfires in the months ahead.
